file.content:
# justfile — Hypera Charlie ArgoCD project
# Task runner for the charlie-server Kustomize/Kubernetes/ArgoCD workflow.
# Run `just` or `just help` to list all recipes.

set shell := ["bash", "-eu", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]
set dotenv-load := false

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Variables (override on the CLI, e.g. `just namespace=charlie-dev apply`)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

namespace := "charlie-prd"
app := "charlie-server"
image := "acrcharliehypera.azurecr.io/charlie-server"
tag := "v0.0.1"
context := ""
argocd_server := env_var_or_default("ARGOCD_SERVER", "")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Help (default)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Show available recipes
default: help

help:
    @just --list --unsorted

# Print resolved variables
vars:
    @echo "namespace     = {{ namespace }}"
    @echo "app           = {{ app }}"
    @echo "image         = {{ image }}"
    @echo "tag           = {{ tag }}"
    @echo "context       = {{ context }}"
    @echo "argocd_server = {{ argocd_server }}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Setup
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Install pre-commit hooks (commit + push stages)
install-hooks:
    pre-commit install --hook-type pre-commit --hook-type pre-push

# Uninstall pre-commit hooks
uninstall-hooks:
    pre-commit uninstall --hook-type pre-commit --hook-type pre-push

# Update pre-commit hook versions
update-hooks:
    pre-commit autoupdate

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Formatting & linting
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Format this justfile (in place)
fmt:
    just --fmt --unstable

# Check justfile formatting without writing
fmt-check:
    just --fmt --unstable --check

# Lint all YAML files (matches .pre-commit-config.yaml yamllint rules)
lint:
    yamllint --strict -d '{rules: {line-length: {max: 180}}}' .

# Run pre-commit on staged files (mirrors `make pre-commit`)
pre-commit:
    pre-commit run --hook-stage commit

# Run pre-commit on all files at push stage (mirrors `make pre-push`)
pre-push:
    pre-commit run --hook-stage push --all-files

# Run every pre-commit hook against every file
pre-commit-all:
    pre-commit run --all-files

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Kustomize / build
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Render the Kustomize overlay to stdout
build:
    kustomize build .

# Render the overlay into ./rendered.yaml
build-out:
    kustomize build . -o rendered.yaml
    @echo "Wrote rendered.yaml"

# Show the resolved images block
show-images:
    kustomize build . | grep -E '^\s+image:' | sort -u

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Validation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Offline validation: kustomize builds cleanly
validate:
    kustomize build . >/dev/null && echo "kustomize build OK"

# kubectl client-side dry run (requires cluster for API discovery)
validate-client:
    kustomize build . | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -n {{ namespace }} -f -

# kubectl server-side dry run (requires cluster access)
validate-server:
    kustomize build . | kubectl apply --dry-run=server -n {{ namespace }} -f -

# Show the diff vs. the live cluster (exit 1 means drift present)
diff:
    kustomize build . | kubectl diff -n {{ namespace }} -f - || true

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Deploy (cluster-level; require explicit context where relevant)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Ensure the target namespace exists
ensure-namespace:
    kubectl get namespace {{ namespace }} >/dev/null 2>&1 \
      || kubectl create namespace {{ namespace }}

# Apply manifests to the target namespace
apply: ensure-namespace
    kustomize build . | kubectl apply -n {{ namespace }} -f -

# Delete manifests from the target namespace
delete:
    kustomize build . | kubectl delete --ignore-not-found -n {{ namespace }} -f -

# Restart the charlie-server deployment
restart:
    kubectl -n {{ namespace }} rollout restart deployment/{{ app }}-deploy

# Wait for the deployment to become available
wait:
    kubectl -n {{ namespace }} rollout status deployment/{{ app }}-deploy --timeout=180s

# Tail logs for all app pods
logs replicas="1":
    kubectl -n {{ namespace }} logs -l app={{ app }} --tail={{ replicas }}00 -f

# Describe the deployment
describe:
    kubectl -n {{ namespace }} describe deployment/{{ app }}-deploy

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Image management
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Set the image tag in kustomization.yaml (e.g. `just set-image v0.0.2`)
set-image new_tag:
    kustomize edit set image {{ image }}={{ image }}:{{ new_tag }}
    @echo "Image updated: {{ image }}:{{ new_tag }}"

# Print the current image tag from kustomization.yaml
current-image:
    @grep -A1 'name: {{ image }}' kustomization.yaml | tail -n1 | tr -d ' '

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ArgoCD
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Show ArgoCD application status
argocd-status:
    argocd app get {{ app }}

# Sync the ArgoCD application
argocd-sync:
    argocd app sync {{ app }}

# Hard refresh the ArgoCD application (re-read git)
argocd-refresh:
    argocd app get {{ app }} --hard-refresh

# Show ArgoCD application diff vs live state
argocd-diff:
    argocd app diff {{ app }} || true

# Show ArgoCD application history
argocd-history:
    argocd app history {{ app }}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Secret helpers (inspection only; never prints decoded values by default)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# List secrets defined in the overlay
list-secrets:
    kustomize build . | awk '/^kind: Secret$/{f=1;next} f && /^  name:/{print $2; f=0}'

# Show metadata for the ACR auth secret
show-acr-auth:
    kubectl -n {{ namespace }} get secret acr-azure-auth -o yaml \
      | grep -vE '^\s*(\.dockerconfigjson|tls\.crt|tls\.key):'

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Aggregate entry points
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Full local CI: lint + format check + validate
ci: fmt-check lint validate

# Clean generated artifacts
clean:
    rm -f rendered.yaml

23	## [acr-auth.yaml](./acr-auth.yaml) Description
24	
25	`acr-auth.yaml` is a Kubernetes Secret configuration file. It contains the Docker registry authentication information for Azure Container Registry (ACR). The `data.dockerconfigjson` field contains a base64 encoded authentication token. This secret can be used to pull images from the ACR in a Kubernetes cluster.
26	
27	## [deployment.yaml](./deployment.yaml) Description
28	
29	`deployment.yaml` is a Kubernetes Deployment configuration file. It defines a Deployment for the `charlie-server` application. The Deployment specifies that there should be one replica of the `charlie-server` container, which is pulled from the `acrcharliehypera.azurecr.io/charlie-server:v0.0.1-dev` image in the ACR. The `acr-azure-auth` Secret is used to authenticate to the ACR. The container is configured to always pull the image (even if it already exists locally), to limit its CPU usage to 200m and its memory usage to 256M, and to expose ports 80 and 443.
30	
31	## [hypera-ca-secret.yaml](./hypera-ca-secret.yaml) Description
32	
33	`hypera-ca-secret.yaml` is a Kubernetes Secret configuration file. It contains the TLS certificate and key for the Hypera CA. The `data.tls.crt` and `data.tls.key` fields contain the base64 encoded certificate and key, respectively. This secret can be used to establish secure connections to services in the `charlie-prd` namespace.
34	
35	## [ingress-web.yaml](./ingress-web.yaml) Description
36	
37	`ingress-web.yaml` is a Traefik IngressRoute configuration file. It defines an IngressRoute for the `charlie-server` application. The IngressRoute specifies that traffic coming to `automacao.hypera.com.br` on the `web` entrypoint should be routed to the `charlie-server-service` on port 80. The `redirect-to-https` middleware is applied to redirect HTTP traffic to HTTPS.
38	
39	## [ingress-websecure.yaml](./ingress-websecure.yaml) Description
40	
41	`ingress-websecure.yaml` is a Traefik IngressRoute configuration file. It defines an IngressRoute for the `charlie-server` application for secure web traffic. The IngressRoute specifies that traffic coming to `automacao.hypera.com.br` on the `websecure` entrypoint should be routed to the `charlie-server-service` on port 80. The `hypera-ca-secret` Secret is used for TLS termination.
42	
43	## [kustomization.yaml](./kustomization.yaml) Description
44	
45	`kustomization.yaml` is a configuration file for Kustomize, a Kubernetes-native configuration management tool. It specifies a set of resources to be created in a Kubernetes cluster and allows for customization of these resources without modifying their original configuration files. In this project, it includes the `acr-auth.yaml`, `deployment.yaml`, `ingress-web.yaml`, `ingress-websecure.yaml`, `redirect-to-https.yaml`, and `service.yaml` resources. It also specifies a common label to be added to all resources and updates the tag of the `acrcharliehypera.azurecr.io/charlie-server` image.
46	
47	## [redirect-to-https.yaml](./redirect-to-https.yaml) Description
48	
49	`redirect-to-https.yaml` is a Traefik Middleware configuration file. It defines a Middleware that redirects HTTP traffic to HTTPS. The Middleware is used in the `ingress-web.yaml` IngressRoute to ensure that all traffic to the `charlie-server` application is secure.
50	
51	## [service.yaml](./service.yaml) Description
52	
53	`service.yaml` is a Kubernetes Service configuration file. It defines a Service for the `charlie-server` application. The Service exposes the `charlie-server` application on ports 80 and 443 and routes traffic to the `charlie-server` Pods. The Service uses the `ClientIP` session affinity mode to ensure that all requests from a client are directed to the same Pod.
54

file.content:
# Project Name

## Description

A brief description of what this project does and who it's for.

## Installation 

Provide detailed instructions about how to install this project.

## Usage 

Explain how to use this project after installation.

## Contributing

Details about how to contribute to this project.

## License

Information about the project's license.

## [acr-auth.yaml](./acr-auth.yaml) Description

`acr-auth.yaml` is a Kubernetes Secret configuration file. It contains the Docker registry authentication information for Azure Container Registry (ACR). The `data.dockerconfigjson` field contains a base64 encoded authentication token. This secret can be used to pull images from the ACR in a Kubernetes cluster.

## [deployment.yaml](./deployment.yaml) Description

`deployment.yaml` is a Kubernetes Deployment configuration file. It defines a Deployment for the `charlie-server` application. The Deployment specifies that there should be one replica of the `charlie-server` container, which is pulled from the `acrcharliehypera.azurecr.io/charlie-server:v0.0.1-dev` image in the ACR. The `acr-azure-auth` Secret is used to authenticate to the ACR. The container is configured to always pull the image (even if it already exists locally), to limit its CPU usage to 200m and its memory usage to 256M, and to expose ports 80 and 443.

## [hypera-ca-secret.yaml](./hypera-ca-secret.yaml) Description

`hypera-ca-secret.yaml` is a Kubernetes Secret configuration file. It contains the TLS certificate and key for the Hypera CA. The `data.tls.crt` and `data.tls.key` fields contain the base64 encoded certificate and key, respectively. This secret can be used to establish secure connections to services in the `charlie-prd` namespace.

## [ingress-web.yaml](./ingress-web.yaml) Description

`ingress-web.yaml` is a Traefik IngressRoute configuration file. It defines an IngressRoute for the `charlie-server` application. The IngressRoute specifies that traffic coming to `automacao.hypera.com.br` on the `web` entrypoint should be routed to the `charlie-server-service` on port 80. The `redirect-to-https` middleware is applied to redirect HTTP traffic to HTTPS.

## [ingress-websecure.yaml](./ingress-websecure.yaml) Description

`ingress-websecure.yaml` is a Traefik IngressRoute configuration file. It defines an IngressRoute for the `charlie-server` application for secure web traffic. The IngressRoute specifies that traffic coming to `automacao.hypera.com.br` on the `websecure` entrypoint should be routed to the `charlie-server-service` on port 80. The `hypera-ca-secret` Secret is used for TLS termination.

## [kustomization.yaml](./kustomization.yaml) Description

`kustomization.yaml` is a configuration file for Kustomize, a Kubernetes-native configuration management tool. It specifies a set of resources to be created in a Kubernetes cluster and allows for customization of these resources without modifying their original configuration files. In this project, it includes the `acr-auth.yaml`, `deployment.yaml`, `ingress-web.yaml`, `ingress-websecure.yaml`, `redirect-to-https.yaml`, and `service.yaml` resources. It also specifies a common label to be added to all resources and updates the tag of the `acrcharliehypera.azurecr.io/charlie-server` image.

## [redirect-to-https.yaml](./redirect-to-https.yaml) Description

`redirect-to-https.yaml` is a Traefik Middleware configuration file. It defines a Middleware that redirects HTTP traffic to HTTPS. The Middleware is used in the `ingress-web.yaml` IngressRoute to ensure that all traffic to the `charlie-server` application is secure.

## [service.yaml](./service.yaml) Description

`service.yaml` is a Kubernetes Service configuration file. It defines a Service for the `charlie-server` application. The Service exposes the `charlie-server` application on ports 80 and 443 and routes traffic to the `charlie-server` Pods. The Service uses the `ClientIP` session affinity mode to ensure that all requests from a client are directed to the same Pod.

python3 -c "
import yaml, base64
with open('hypera-ca-secret.yaml') as f:
    d = yaml.safe_load(f)
crt = base64.b64decode(d['data']['tls.crt']).decode()
print(crt[:200])
print('...')
print('length:', len(crt))
print('BEGIN CERT count:', crt.count('BEGIN CERTIFICATE'))
with open('/tmp/existing_secret.crt','w') as f: f.write(crt)
"
echo "---"
openssl x509 -in /tmp/existing_secret.crt -noout -subject -issuer -startdate -enddate 2>&1
echo "--- private key in secret ---"
python3 -c "
import yaml, base64
with open('hypera-ca-secret.yaml') as f:
    d = yaml.safe_load(f)
key = base64.b64decode(d['data']['tls.key']).decode()
print(key[:100])
print('length:', len(key))
with open('/tmp/existing_secret.key','w') as f: f.write(key)
"

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture. Critical findings:

- **Existing cert in secret**: valid `2025-10-30 → 2026-05-11` (expires **~3 weeks from today**)
- **New cert in `certs/current/`**: valid `2026-04-16 → 2026-11-01` (new wildcard `*.hypera.com.br`)
- Same CN, same issuer (GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018), renewed for 6 months

Let me check the PFX passphrase situation and whether the key in the bundle matches before drafting the plan.

input.command:
awk '/-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----/,/-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----/' certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem > /tmp/new.key
awk '/-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----/{n++} n==1' certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem | awk '/-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----/,/-----END CERTIFICATE-----/' > /tmp/new.crt
head -1 /tmp/new.key
head -1 /tmp/new.crt
echo "--- key pub hash ---"
openssl rsa -in /tmp/new.key -pubout 2>/dev/null | openssl md5
echo "--- cert pub hash ---"
openssl x509 -in /tmp/new.crt -pubkey -noout | openssl md5
echo "--- second cert (chain) ---"
awk '/-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----/{n++} n==2,/-----END CERTIFICATE-----/ && n==2' certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem | openssl x509 -noout -subject 2>&1
echo "--- third cert (chain) ---"
awk 'BEGIN{n=0} /-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----/{n++} n==3' certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem | openssl x509 -noout -subject 2>&1
input.description: Verify key matches cert + inspect chain

Let me retry cleanly, and verify key↔cert match, then check one more critical thing: `certs/` is NOT in `.gitignore`, so I need to warn about secret material.

Two important findings:

1. **Modulus mismatch** between key and cert — but this might be my extraction being wrong. The PEM file has structure: line 8-37 is the RSA key, lines 43-89 is cert #1, then cert #2 (89-113), cert #3 (118-143). Wait — my sed extracted both keys (lines 30 in a file that's only 143 lines). There might be a second private key at the end. Let me verify.

2. **Certs ARE gitignored globally** (`.pem` in global gitignore) — good, they won't accidentally be committed.

input.content:
# Plan — Rotate `hypera-ca-secret` to new 2026 wildcard certificate

## Context

The current TLS secret `hypera-ca-secret` terminates HTTPS for `automacao.hypera.com.br` via Traefik IngressRoute `charlie-server-websecure` (see `ingress-websecure.yaml:10`). The embedded certificate expires **2026-05-11** (≈21 days from today). A new wildcard cert `*.hypera.com.br` valid **2026-04-16 → 2026-11-01** has been delivered to `certs/current/` and must replace the in-repo secret so ArgoCD propagates it to the `charlie-prd` namespace.

**Why now:** the current cert expires in 3 weeks. We rotate in git so ArgoCD syncs the new secret to the cluster; Traefik picks up the updated secret automatically.

**Intended outcome:** `hypera-ca-secret.yaml` contains the new cert (chain) + matching unencrypted key as base64, commits cleanly to main, ArgoCD syncs, and `automacao.hypera.com.br` serves the new cert with `notAfter=Nov 1 2026`.

---

## Pre-flight facts verified

| Item | Current (decoded from `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`) | New (in `certs/current/`) |
|---|---|---|
| Subject CN | `*.hypera.com.br` | `*.hypera.com.br` ✅ same |
| Issuer | GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 | same ✅ |
| notBefore | 2025-10-30 | 2026-04-16 |
| notAfter | **2026-05-11** ⚠️ | 2026-11-01 |
| Key | Unencrypted RSA (PKCS#1) | **Encrypted** DES-EDE3-CBC (passphrase required) |

- Secret consumer: `ingress-websecure.yaml:10` only.
- Namespace (justfile default): `charlie-prd`.
- `*.pem` is in global `.gitignore` — cert source files won't be committed.
- `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem` contains: 1 encrypted RSA key + 3 certs (leaf, GlobalSign root, GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 intermediate).
- `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx` is PKCS#12, also password-protected.

---

## ⚠️ Blocker — passphrase required

The new private key is encrypted. **Nothing can proceed without the cert passphrase.** I will ask for it at step 1 and accept it via interactive prompt (never take it via chat text / never store it).

---

## Execution plan (step-by-step, you drive, I assist)

### Step 1 — Obtain & stage the unencrypted key + cert chain (LOCAL ONLY, secrets stay out of repo)

Work in a scratch dir outside the repo (e.g. `~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/`) to avoid any risk of committing secrets.

```bash
mkdir -p ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026
cd ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026

# Extract leaf certificate (first block in the .pem)
openssl x509 -in /absolute/path/to/certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem \
  -out leaf.crt

# Extract + decrypt private key (will prompt for passphrase)
openssl rsa \
  -in /absolute/path/to/certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem \
  -out leaf.key
# ^ prompts: "Enter pass phrase for …:"

# OR, equivalent extraction from the PFX:
# openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -nocerts -nodes -out leaf.key
# openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -clcerts -nokeys -out leaf.crt

# Build chain = leaf + intermediate (GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 = CACert.crt)
cat leaf.crt /absolute/path/to/certs/current/CACert.crt > fullchain.crt
```

**Verification (MUST pass before step 2):**
```bash
# Modulus match — key ↔ cert are a pair
diff <(openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -modulus | openssl md5) \
     <(openssl rsa  -in leaf.key  -noout -modulus | openssl md5)
# (no output = match)

# Dates and CN sanity
openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates
# expect: subject=*.hypera.com.br, notAfter=Nov 1 2026

# Chain validates
openssl verify -CAfile /absolute/path/to/certs/current/root.cer \
  -untrusted /absolute/path/to/certs/current/CACert.crt leaf.crt
# expect: leaf.crt: OK
```

### Step 2 — Generate the new `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`

From the repo worktree root. This overwrites the existing file with a dry-run–rendered Secret using the new artifacts.

```bash
kubectl create secret tls hypera-ca-secret \
  --cert=$HOME/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/fullchain.crt \
  --key=$HOME/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/leaf.key \
  --dry-run=client \
  -o yaml > hypera-ca-secret.yaml
```

Ensure the resulting file matches the existing structure:
- `apiVersion: v1`, `kind: Secret`, `type: kubernetes.io/tls`
- `metadata.name: hypera-ca-secret` (no namespace — applied via Kustomize/ArgoCD)

**Note on `type`:** current file is `Opaque`; `kubectl create secret tls` emits `kubernetes.io/tls`. Traefik's `IngressRoute.tls.secretName` works with either, but `kubernetes.io/tls` is the correct/idiomatic type and requires the keys to be named exactly `tls.crt` / `tls.key` (which both are). **Recommend switching to `kubernetes.io/tls`** — it's a strict upgrade and matches Kubernetes conventions. Confirm with you before the switch.

### Step 3 — Local validation (no cluster impact)

```bash
just validate                # kustomize build . OK
just build | yq 'select(.kind=="Secret" and .metadata.name=="hypera-ca-secret") | .data."tls.crt"' \
  | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026
```

### Step 4 — Commit & push (secrets committed to git ARE expected here; this is the existing pattern)

```bash
git status          # only hypera-ca-secret.yaml should appear
git add hypera-ca-secret.yaml
git commit -m "chore(tls): rotate hypera-ca-secret to 2026-11-01 wildcard cert"
git push
```

This repo already stores the TLS secret in plaintext git — we're matching existing practice. **Separately, we should open a follow-up to adopt SealedSecrets / SOPS / External Secrets** (noted in Risks, not part of this rotation).

### Step 5 — ArgoCD sync & verification

```bash
just argocd-refresh      # hard-refresh git
just argocd-diff         # expect diff only on hypera-ca-secret data
just argocd-sync         # apply
just argocd-status       # Synced / Healthy
```

### Step 6 — Post-deploy verification

```bash
# From inside cluster or wherever you have access
kubectl -n charlie-prd get secret hypera-ca-secret \
  -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.crt}' | base64 -d | \
  openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026

# From outside — real TLS handshake
echo | openssl s_client -servername automacao.hypera.com.br \
  -connect automacao.hypera.com.br:443 2>/dev/null | \
  openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026
```

Traefik auto-reloads cert material on Secret changes; no pod restart required. If the served cert doesn't flip within ~60s, check the Traefik pod logs for reload events.

### Step 7 — Cleanup

```bash
shred -u ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/leaf.key  # or `rm -P` on macOS
rm -rf ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026
```

---

## Files modified

| Path | Change |
|---|---|
| `hypera-ca-secret.yaml` | Overwrite `data.tls.crt` (full chain) and `data.tls.key`; optionally flip `type` from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` |

No other files change. `ingress-websecure.yaml`, `kustomization.yaml`, and `deployment.yaml` remain untouched because the secret name is unchanged.

---

## Existing utilities reused

- `just validate` — offline kustomize build check (justfile:104)
- `just argocd-refresh` / `argocd-sync` / `argocd-status` / `argocd-diff` (justfile:178, 174, 170, 182)
- `just list-secrets` — sanity check that the secret is still included in the overlay (justfile:194)

No new scripts or recipes needed.

---

## Risks

1. **Downtime window is the critical variable.** Current cert expires 2026-05-11 — 21 days. Rotation must land before then. Surgical single-file change minimizes blast radius.
2. **Secret material in plain git.** Pre-existing pattern; rotation doesn't worsen it but doesn't fix it. Out-of-scope follow-up: SealedSecrets or External Secrets Operator.
3. **Wrong key/cert pairing** — mitigated by modulus-match check in Step 1.
4. **Missing intermediate in chain** — browsers/clients may reject leaf without `GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018`. We explicitly concatenate `CACert.crt` into the served chain.
5. **Passphrase exposure** — handled by `openssl rsa` interactive prompt; no chat transport of the secret.
6. **ArgoCD auto-sync behavior** — if auto-sync with `selfHeal` is on, step 5's manual sync is cosmetic. If prune/sync waves are configured, confirm the Secret is in the expected wave.

---

## Verification (end-to-end success criteria)

- `openssl x509 … notAfter` = `Nov 1 20:46:17 2026 GMT` on the decoded secret and on the live endpoint.
- `openssl verify` succeeds with `root.cer` trust anchor and `CACert.crt` intermediate.
- ArgoCD shows `Synced` and `Healthy` for the `charlie-server` application.
- `https://automacao.hypera.com.br` handshake succeeds with the new cert's dates.
- No churn in any file other than `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`.

---

## Open questions for you before I proceed

1. Do you have the **private key passphrase** ready (or shall we extract from `.pfx` instead)?
2. OK to switch the secret `type` from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` (recommended), or keep it `Opaque` to minimize diff?
3. Is ArgoCD **auto-sync** enabled for `charlie-server`? (Changes whether Step 5 is required or cosmetic.)
4. Preferred moment to flip: straight after commit, or scheduled maintenance window?

lastPrompt: we have notice about my company change cert @certs/current we need your assistance step by step to change this workload to this new cert

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/juliano.barbosa/Repos/azure/hypera-rg-hypera-padhy/Repos/hypera-charlie-argocd/.claude/worktrees/wt-change-cert-hypera-to-2026/Plans/we-have-notice-about-adaptive-forest.md

content.text: I need four clarifying answers before I can finalize execution — let me ask them now via AskUserQuestion, then exit plan mode.

questions.question: How will you supply the private key for the new certificate?

options.label: Decrypt the .pem key (I have the passphrase)
options.description: You run `openssl rsa -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem -out leaf.key` and enter the passphrase interactively. Recommended — simplest path.

options.label: Extract from the .pfx
options.description: You run `openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -nocerts -nodes -out leaf.key` with the PFX password. Use if you only have the .pfx passphrase.

options.label: I don't have the passphrase yet
options.description: We pause the rotation until you obtain it from whoever issued the cert. Cannot proceed without it.

questions.question: Should the rotated Secret keep type Opaque or switch to kubernetes.io/tls?

options.label: Switch to kubernetes.io/tls (Recommended)
options.description: Idiomatic Kubernetes TLS type; Traefik treats it identically. `kubectl create secret tls` emits this natively. One-line diff on top of the data rotation.

options.label: Keep type: Opaque
options.description: Minimizes diff — only tls.crt and tls.key base64 change. Slightly less idiomatic but zero risk of behavioral surprise.

questions.question: Is ArgoCD auto-sync enabled for the charlie-server application?

options.label: Yes — auto-sync is on
options.description: Commit + push is enough; ArgoCD will reconcile automatically. Manual `argocd-sync` becomes a cosmetic nudge.

options.label: No — manual sync required
options.description: We'll explicitly `just argocd-refresh && just argocd-sync` after push.

options.label: I'm not sure
options.description: I'll run `argocd app get charlie-server` (or `just argocd-status`) first to check before committing.

# Plan — Rotate `hypera-ca-secret` to new 2026 wildcard certificate

## Context

The current TLS secret `hypera-ca-secret` terminates HTTPS for `automacao.hypera.com.br` via Traefik IngressRoute `charlie-server-websecure` (see `ingress-websecure.yaml:10`). The embedded certificate expires **2026-05-11** (≈21 days from today). A new wildcard cert `*.hypera.com.br` valid **2026-04-16 → 2026-11-01** has been delivered to `certs/current/` and must replace the in-repo secret so ArgoCD propagates it to the `charlie-prd` namespace.

**Why now:** the current cert expires in 3 weeks. We rotate in git so ArgoCD syncs the new secret to the cluster; Traefik picks up the updated secret automatically.

**Intended outcome:** `hypera-ca-secret.yaml` contains the new cert (chain) + matching unencrypted key as base64, commits cleanly to main, ArgoCD syncs, and `automacao.hypera.com.br` serves the new cert with `notAfter=Nov 1 2026`.

---

## Pre-flight facts verified

| Item | Current (decoded from `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`) | New (in `certs/current/`) |
|---|---|---|
| Subject CN | `*.hypera.com.br` | `*.hypera.com.br` ✅ same |
| Issuer | GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 | same ✅ |
| notBefore | 2025-10-30 | 2026-04-16 |
| notAfter | **2026-05-11** ⚠️ | 2026-11-01 |
| Key | Unencrypted RSA (PKCS#1) | **Encrypted** DES-EDE3-CBC (passphrase required) |

- Secret consumer: `ingress-websecure.yaml:10` only.
- Namespace (justfile default): `charlie-prd`.
- `*.pem` is in global `.gitignore` — cert source files won't be committed.
- `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem` contains: 1 encrypted RSA key + 3 certs (leaf, GlobalSign root, GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 intermediate).
- `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx` is PKCS#12, also password-protected.

---

## ⚠️ Blocker — passphrase required

The new private key is encrypted. **Nothing can proceed without the cert passphrase.** I will ask for it at step 1 and accept it via interactive prompt (never take it via chat text / never store it).

---

## Execution plan (step-by-step, you drive, I assist)

### Step 1 — Obtain & stage the unencrypted key + cert chain (LOCAL ONLY, secrets stay out of repo)

Work in a scratch dir outside the repo (e.g. `~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/`) to avoid any risk of committing secrets.

```bash
mkdir -p ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026
cd ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026

# Extract leaf certificate (first block in the .pem)
openssl x509 -in /absolute/path/to/certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem \
  -out leaf.crt

# Extract + decrypt private key (will prompt for passphrase)
openssl rsa \
  -in /absolute/path/to/certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem \
  -out leaf.key
# ^ prompts: "Enter pass phrase for …:"

# OR, equivalent extraction from the PFX:
# openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -nocerts -nodes -out leaf.key
# openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -clcerts -nokeys -out leaf.crt

# Build chain = leaf + intermediate (GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 = CACert.crt)
cat leaf.crt /absolute/path/to/certs/current/CACert.crt > fullchain.crt
```

**Verification (MUST pass before step 2):**
```bash
# Modulus match — key ↔ cert are a pair
diff <(openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -modulus | openssl md5) \
     <(openssl rsa  -in leaf.key  -noout -modulus | openssl md5)
# (no output = match)

# Dates and CN sanity
openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates
# expect: subject=*.hypera.com.br, notAfter=Nov 1 2026

# Chain validates
openssl verify -CAfile /absolute/path/to/certs/current/root.cer \
  -untrusted /absolute/path/to/certs/current/CACert.crt leaf.crt
# expect: leaf.crt: OK
```

### Step 2 — Generate the new `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`

From the repo worktree root. This overwrites the existing file with a dry-run–rendered Secret using the new artifacts.

```bash
kubectl create secret tls hypera-ca-secret \
  --cert=$HOME/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/fullchain.crt \
  --key=$HOME/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/leaf.key \
  --dry-run=client \
  -o yaml > hypera-ca-secret.yaml
```

Ensure the resulting file matches the existing structure:
- `apiVersion: v1`, `kind: Secret`, `type: kubernetes.io/tls`
- `metadata.name: hypera-ca-secret` (no namespace — applied via Kustomize/ArgoCD)

**Note on `type`:** current file is `Opaque`; `kubectl create secret tls` emits `kubernetes.io/tls`. Traefik's `IngressRoute.tls.secretName` works with either, but `kubernetes.io/tls` is the correct/idiomatic type and requires the keys to be named exactly `tls.crt` / `tls.key` (which both are). **Recommend switching to `kubernetes.io/tls`** — it's a strict upgrade and matches Kubernetes conventions. Confirm with you before the switch.

### Step 3 — Local validation (no cluster impact)

```bash
just validate                # kustomize build . OK
just build | yq 'select(.kind=="Secret" and .metadata.name=="hypera-ca-secret") | .data."tls.crt"' \
  | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026
```

### Step 4 — Commit & push (secrets committed to git ARE expected here; this is the existing pattern)

```bash
git status          # only hypera-ca-secret.yaml should appear
git add hypera-ca-secret.yaml
git commit -m "chore(tls): rotate hypera-ca-secret to 2026-11-01 wildcard cert"
git push
```

This repo already stores the TLS secret in plaintext git — we're matching existing practice. **Separately, we should open a follow-up to adopt SealedSecrets / SOPS / External Secrets** (noted in Risks, not part of this rotation).

### Step 5 — ArgoCD sync & verification

```bash
just argocd-refresh      # hard-refresh git
just argocd-diff         # expect diff only on hypera-ca-secret data
just argocd-sync         # apply
just argocd-status       # Synced / Healthy
```

### Step 6 — Post-deploy verification

```bash
# From inside cluster or wherever you have access
kubectl -n charlie-prd get secret hypera-ca-secret \
  -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.crt}' | base64 -d | \
  openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026

# From outside — real TLS handshake
echo | openssl s_client -servername automacao.hypera.com.br \
  -connect automacao.hypera.com.br:443 2>/dev/null | \
  openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026
```

Traefik auto-reloads cert material on Secret changes; no pod restart required. If the served cert doesn't flip within ~60s, check the Traefik pod logs for reload events.

### Step 7 — Cleanup

```bash
shred -u ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/leaf.key  # or `rm -P` on macOS
rm -rf ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026
```

---

## Files modified

| Path | Change |
|---|---|
| `hypera-ca-secret.yaml` | Overwrite `data.tls.crt` (full chain) and `data.tls.key`; optionally flip `type` from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` |

No other files change. `ingress-websecure.yaml`, `kustomization.yaml`, and `deployment.yaml` remain untouched because the secret name is unchanged.

---

## Existing utilities reused

- `just validate` — offline kustomize build check (justfile:104)
- `just argocd-refresh` / `argocd-sync` / `argocd-status` / `argocd-diff` (justfile:178, 174, 170, 182)
- `just list-secrets` — sanity check that the secret is still included in the overlay (justfile:194)

No new scripts or recipes needed.

---

## Risks

1. **Downtime window is the critical variable.** Current cert expires 2026-05-11 — 21 days. Rotation must land before then. Surgical single-file change minimizes blast radius.
2. **Secret material in plain git.** Pre-existing pattern; rotation doesn't worsen it but doesn't fix it. Out-of-scope follow-up: SealedSecrets or External Secrets Operator.
3. **Wrong key/cert pairing** — mitigated by modulus-match check in Step 1.
4. **Missing intermediate in chain** — browsers/clients may reject leaf without `GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018`. We explicitly concatenate `CACert.crt` into the served chain.
5. **Passphrase exposure** — handled by `openssl rsa` interactive prompt; no chat transport of the secret.
6. **ArgoCD auto-sync behavior** — if auto-sync with `selfHeal` is on, step 5's manual sync is cosmetic. If prune/sync waves are configured, confirm the Secret is in the expected wave.

---

## Verification (end-to-end success criteria)

- `openssl x509 … notAfter` = `Nov 1 20:46:17 2026 GMT` on the decoded secret and on the live endpoint.
- `openssl verify` succeeds with `root.cer` trust anchor and `CACert.crt` intermediate.
- ArgoCD shows `Synced` and `Healthy` for the `charlie-server` application.
- `https://automacao.hypera.com.br` handshake succeeds with the new cert's dates.
- No churn in any file other than `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`.

---

## Open questions for you before I proceed

1. Do you have the **private key passphrase** ready (or shall we extract from `.pfx` instead)?
2. OK to switch the secret `type` from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` (recommended), or keep it `Opaque` to minimize diff?
3. Is ArgoCD **auto-sync** enabled for `charlie-server`? (Changes whether Step 5 is required or cosmetic.)
4. Preferred moment to flip: straight after commit, or scheduled maintenance window?

# Plan — Rotate `hypera-ca-secret` to new 2026 wildcard certificate

## Context

The current TLS secret `hypera-ca-secret` terminates HTTPS for `automacao.hypera.com.br` via Traefik IngressRoute `charlie-server-websecure` (see `ingress-websecure.yaml:10`). The embedded certificate expires **2026-05-11** (≈21 days from today). A new wildcard cert `*.hypera.com.br` valid **2026-04-16 → 2026-11-01** has been delivered to `certs/current/` and must replace the in-repo secret so ArgoCD propagates it to the `charlie-prd` namespace.

**Why now:** the current cert expires in 3 weeks. We rotate in git so ArgoCD syncs the new secret to the cluster; Traefik picks up the updated secret automatically.

**Intended outcome:** `hypera-ca-secret.yaml` contains the new cert (chain) + matching unencrypted key as base64, commits cleanly to main, ArgoCD syncs, and `automacao.hypera.com.br` serves the new cert with `notAfter=Nov 1 2026`.

---

## Pre-flight facts verified

| Item | Current (decoded from `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`) | New (in `certs/current/`) |
|---|---|---|
| Subject CN | `*.hypera.com.br` | `*.hypera.com.br` ✅ same |
| Issuer | GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 | same ✅ |
| notBefore | 2025-10-30 | 2026-04-16 |
| notAfter | **2026-05-11** ⚠️ | 2026-11-01 |
| Key | Unencrypted RSA (PKCS#1) | **Encrypted** DES-EDE3-CBC (passphrase required) |

- Secret consumer: `ingress-websecure.yaml:10` only.
- Namespace (justfile default): `charlie-prd`.
- `*.pem` is in global `.gitignore` — cert source files won't be committed.
- `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem` contains: 1 encrypted RSA key + 3 certs (leaf, GlobalSign root, GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 intermediate).
- `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx` is PKCS#12, also password-protected.

---

## Confirmed decisions (from pre-plan Q&A)

1. **Key source:** decrypt `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem` via `openssl rsa` — you have the passphrase and enter it interactively (never via chat).
2. **Secret type:** switch from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` (idiomatic K8s TLS type).
3. **ArgoCD auto-sync:** enabled for `charlie-server` — commit + push is sufficient; manual sync is cosmetic.
4. **Timing:** prepare the repo change today; trigger ArgoCD action during a planned maintenance window (I'll stop after push; you control the sync moment).

---

## Execution plan (step-by-step, you drive, I assist)

### Step 1 — Obtain & stage the unencrypted key + cert chain (LOCAL ONLY, secrets stay out of repo)

Work in a scratch dir outside the repo (e.g. `~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/`) to avoid any risk of committing secrets.

```bash
mkdir -p ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026
cd ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026

# Extract leaf certificate (first block in the .pem)
openssl x509 -in /absolute/path/to/certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem \
  -out leaf.crt

# Extract + decrypt private key (will prompt for passphrase)
openssl rsa \
  -in /absolute/path/to/certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem \
  -out leaf.key
# ^ prompts: "Enter pass phrase for …:"

# OR, equivalent extraction from the PFX:
# openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -nocerts -nodes -out leaf.key
# openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -clcerts -nokeys -out leaf.crt

# Build chain = leaf + intermediate (GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 = CACert.crt)
cat leaf.crt /absolute/path/to/certs/current/CACert.crt > fullchain.crt
```

**Verification (MUST pass before step 2):**
```bash
# Modulus match — key ↔ cert are a pair
diff <(openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -modulus | openssl md5) \
     <(openssl rsa  -in leaf.key  -noout -modulus | openssl md5)
# (no output = match)

# Dates and CN sanity
openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates
# expect: subject=*.hypera.com.br, notAfter=Nov 1 2026

# Chain validates
openssl verify -CAfile /absolute/path/to/certs/current/root.cer \
  -untrusted /absolute/path/to/certs/current/CACert.crt leaf.crt
# expect: leaf.crt: OK
```

### Step 2 — Generate the new `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`

From the repo worktree root. This overwrites the existing file with a dry-run–rendered Secret using the new artifacts.

```bash
kubectl create secret tls hypera-ca-secret \
  --cert=$HOME/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/fullchain.crt \
  --key=$HOME/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/leaf.key \
  --dry-run=client \
  -o yaml > hypera-ca-secret.yaml
```

Resulting file structure (per decision #2):
- `apiVersion: v1`, `kind: Secret`, **`type: kubernetes.io/tls`**
- `metadata.name: hypera-ca-secret` (no namespace — applied via Kustomize/ArgoCD)
- `data.tls.crt`: base64(leaf + `GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018` intermediate)
- `data.tls.key`: base64(unencrypted RSA key)

### Step 3 — Local validation (no cluster impact)

```bash
just validate                # kustomize build . OK
just build | yq 'select(.kind=="Secret" and .metadata.name=="hypera-ca-secret") | .data."tls.crt"' \
  | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026
```

### Step 4 — Commit & push (secrets committed to git ARE expected here; this is the existing pattern)

```bash
git status          # only hypera-ca-secret.yaml should appear
git add hypera-ca-secret.yaml
git commit -m "chore(tls): rotate hypera-ca-secret to 2026-11-01 wildcard cert"
git push
```

This repo already stores the TLS secret in plaintext git — we're matching existing practice. **Separately, we should open a follow-up to adopt SealedSecrets / SOPS / External Secrets** (noted in Risks, not part of this rotation).

### Step 5 — ArgoCD sync & verification

```bash
just argocd-refresh      # hard-refresh git
just argocd-diff         # expect diff only on hypera-ca-secret data
just argocd-sync         # apply
just argocd-status       # Synced / Healthy
```

### Step 6 — Post-deploy verification

```bash
# From inside cluster or wherever you have access
kubectl -n charlie-prd get secret hypera-ca-secret \
  -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.crt}' | base64 -d | \
  openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026

# From outside — real TLS handshake
echo | openssl s_client -servername automacao.hypera.com.br \
  -connect automacao.hypera.com.br:443 2>/dev/null | \
  openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026
```

Traefik auto-reloads cert material on Secret changes; no pod restart required. If the served cert doesn't flip within ~60s, check the Traefik pod logs for reload events.

### Step 7 — Cleanup

```bash
shred -u ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/leaf.key  # or `rm -P` on macOS
rm -rf ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026
```

---

## Files modified

| Path | Change |
|---|---|
| `hypera-ca-secret.yaml` | Overwrite `data.tls.crt` (full chain) and `data.tls.key`; optionally flip `type` from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` |

No other files change. `ingress-websecure.yaml`, `kustomization.yaml`, and `deployment.yaml` remain untouched because the secret name is unchanged.

---

## Existing utilities reused

- `just validate` — offline kustomize build check (justfile:104)
- `just argocd-refresh` / `argocd-sync` / `argocd-status` / `argocd-diff` (justfile:178, 174, 170, 182)
- `just list-secrets` — sanity check that the secret is still included in the overlay (justfile:194)

No new scripts or recipes needed.

---

## Risks

1. **Downtime window is the critical variable.** Current cert expires 2026-05-11 — 21 days. Rotation must land before then. Surgical single-file change minimizes blast radius.
2. **Secret material in plain git.** Pre-existing pattern; rotation doesn't worsen it but doesn't fix it. Out-of-scope follow-up: SealedSecrets or External Secrets Operator.
3. **Wrong key/cert pairing** — mitigated by modulus-match check in Step 1.
4. **Missing intermediate in chain** — browsers/clients may reject leaf without `GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018`. We explicitly concatenate `CACert.crt` into the served chain.
5. **Passphrase exposure** — handled by `openssl rsa` interactive prompt; no chat transport of the secret.
6. **ArgoCD auto-sync behavior** — if auto-sync with `selfHeal` is on, step 5's manual sync is cosmetic. If prune/sync waves are configured, confirm the Secret is in the expected wave.

---

## Verification (end-to-end success criteria)

- `openssl x509 … notAfter` = `Nov 1 20:46:17 2026 GMT` on the decoded secret and on the live endpoint.
- `openssl verify` succeeds with `root.cer` trust anchor and `CACert.crt` intermediate.
- ArgoCD shows `Synced` and `Healthy` for the `charlie-server` application.
- `https://automacao.hypera.com.br` handshake succeeds with the new cert's dates.
- No churn in any file other than `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`.

---

## Open questions for you before I proceed

1. Do you have the **private key passphrase** ready (or shall we extract from `.pfx` instead)?
2. OK to switch the secret `type` from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` (recommended), or keep it `Opaque` to minimize diff?
3. Is ArgoCD **auto-sync** enabled for `charlie-server`? (Changes whether Step 5 is required or cosmetic.)
4. Preferred moment to flip: straight after commit, or scheduled maintenance window?

- `openssl x509 … notAfter` = `Nov 1 20:46:17 2026 GMT` on the decoded secret and on the live endpoint.

- ArgoCD shows `Synced` and `Healthy` for the `charlie-server` application.
- `https://automacao.hypera.com.br` handshake succeeds with the new cert's dates.
- No churn in any file other than `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`.

---

1. Do you have the **private key passphrase** ready (or shall we extract from `.pfx` instead)?
2. OK to switch the secret `type` from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` (recommended), or keep it `Opaque` to minimize diff?
3. Is ArgoCD **auto-sync** enabled for `charlie-server`? (Changes whether Step 5 is required or cosmetic.)
4. Preferred moment to flip: straight after commit, or scheduled maintenance window?

input.plan:
# Plan — Rotate `hypera-ca-secret` to new 2026 wildcard certificate

## Context

The current TLS secret `hypera-ca-secret` terminates HTTPS for `automacao.hypera.com.br` via Traefik IngressRoute `charlie-server-websecure` (see `ingress-websecure.yaml:10`). The embedded certificate expires **2026-05-11** (≈21 days from today). A new wildcard cert `*.hypera.com.br` valid **2026-04-16 → 2026-11-01** has been delivered to `certs/current/` and must replace the in-repo secret so ArgoCD propagates it to the `charlie-prd` namespace.

**Why now:** the current cert expires in 3 weeks. We rotate in git so ArgoCD syncs the new secret to the cluster; Traefik picks up the updated secret automatically.

**Intended outcome:** `hypera-ca-secret.yaml` contains the new cert (chain) + matching unencrypted key as base64, commits cleanly to main, ArgoCD syncs, and `automacao.hypera.com.br` serves the new cert with `notAfter=Nov 1 2026`.

---

## Pre-flight facts verified

| Item | Current (decoded from `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`) | New (in `certs/current/`) |
|---|---|---|
| Subject CN | `*.hypera.com.br` | `*.hypera.com.br` ✅ same |
| Issuer | GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 | same ✅ |
| notBefore | 2025-10-30 | 2026-04-16 |
| notAfter | **2026-05-11** ⚠️ | 2026-11-01 |
| Key | Unencrypted RSA (PKCS#1) | **Encrypted** DES-EDE3-CBC (passphrase required) |

- Secret consumer: `ingress-websecure.yaml:10` only.
- Namespace (justfile default): `charlie-prd`.
- `*.pem` is in global `.gitignore` — cert source files won't be committed.
- `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem` contains: 1 encrypted RSA key + 3 certs (leaf, GlobalSign root, GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 intermediate).
- `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx` is PKCS#12, also password-protected.

---

## Confirmed decisions (from pre-plan Q&A)

1. **Key source:** decrypt `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem` via `openssl rsa` — you have the passphrase and enter it interactively (never via chat).
2. **Secret type:** switch from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` (idiomatic K8s TLS type).
3. **ArgoCD auto-sync:** enabled for `charlie-server` — commit + push is sufficient; manual sync is cosmetic.
4. **Timing:** prepare the repo change today; trigger ArgoCD action during a planned maintenance window (I'll stop after push; you control the sync moment).

---

## Execution plan (step-by-step, you drive, I assist)

### Step 1 — Obtain & stage the unencrypted key + cert chain (LOCAL ONLY, secrets stay out of repo)

Work in a scratch dir outside the repo (e.g. `~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/`) to avoid any risk of committing secrets.

```bash
mkdir -p ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026
cd ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026

# Extract leaf certificate (first block in the .pem)
openssl x509 -in /absolute/path/to/certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem \
  -out leaf.crt

# Extract + decrypt private key (will prompt for passphrase)
openssl rsa \
  -in /absolute/path/to/certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem \
  -out leaf.key
# ^ prompts: "Enter pass phrase for …:"

# OR, equivalent extraction from the PFX:
# openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -nocerts -nodes -out leaf.key
# openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -clcerts -nokeys -out leaf.crt

# Build chain = leaf + intermediate (GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 = CACert.crt)
cat leaf.crt /absolute/path/to/certs/current/CACert.crt > fullchain.crt
```

**Verification (MUST pass before step 2):**
```bash
# Modulus match — key ↔ cert are a pair
diff <(openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -modulus | openssl md5) \
     <(openssl rsa  -in leaf.key  -noout -modulus | openssl md5)
# (no output = match)

# Dates and CN sanity
openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates
# expect: subject=*.hypera.com.br, notAfter=Nov 1 2026

# Chain validates
openssl verify -CAfile /absolute/path/to/certs/current/root.cer \
  -untrusted /absolute/path/to/certs/current/CACert.crt leaf.crt
# expect: leaf.crt: OK
```

### Step 2 — Generate the new `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`

From the repo worktree root. This overwrites the existing file with a dry-run–rendered Secret using the new artifacts.

```bash
kubectl create secret tls hypera-ca-secret \
  --cert=$HOME/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/fullchain.crt \
  --key=$HOME/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/leaf.key \
  --dry-run=client \
  -o yaml > hypera-ca-secret.yaml
```

Resulting file structure (per decision #2):
- `apiVersion: v1`, `kind: Secret`, **`type: kubernetes.io/tls`**
- `metadata.name: hypera-ca-secret` (no namespace — applied via Kustomize/ArgoCD)
- `data.tls.crt`: base64(leaf + `GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018` intermediate)
- `data.tls.key`: base64(unencrypted RSA key)

### Step 3 — Local validation (no cluster impact)

```bash
just validate                # kustomize build . OK
just build | yq 'select(.kind=="Secret" and .metadata.name=="hypera-ca-secret") | .data."tls.crt"' \
  | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026
```

### Step 4 — Commit & push (secrets committed to git ARE expected here; this is the existing pattern)

```bash
git status          # only hypera-ca-secret.yaml should appear
git add hypera-ca-secret.yaml
git commit -m "chore(tls): rotate hypera-ca-secret to 2026-11-01 wildcard cert"
git push
```

This repo already stores the TLS secret in plaintext git — we're matching existing practice. **Separately, we should open a follow-up to adopt SealedSecrets / SOPS / External Secrets** (noted in Risks, not part of this rotation).

### Step 5 — ArgoCD reconciliation (during your maintenance window)

Auto-sync is enabled — **no action needed from me after push**. During your maintenance window you can:

```bash
just argocd-status       # check it reconciled; expect Synced / Healthy
# Optional nudge if you don't want to wait for the next reconcile tick:
just argocd-refresh
just argocd-diff         # expect only hypera-ca-secret data changed
```

### Step 6 — Post-deploy verification (during your maintenance window)

```bash
# From inside cluster or wherever you have access
kubectl -n charlie-prd get secret hypera-ca-secret \
  -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.crt}' | base64 -d | \
  openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026

# From outside — real TLS handshake
echo | openssl s_client -servername automacao.hypera.com.br \
  -connect automacao.hypera.com.br:443 2>/dev/null | \
  openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026
```

Traefik auto-reloads cert material on Secret changes; no pod restart required. If the served cert doesn't flip within ~60s, check the Traefik pod logs for reload events.

### Step 7 — Cleanup

```bash
shred -u ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/leaf.key  # or `rm -P` on macOS
rm -rf ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026
```

---

## Files modified

| Path | Change |
|---|---|
| `hypera-ca-secret.yaml` | Overwrite `data.tls.crt` (full chain) and `data.tls.key`; optionally flip `type` from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` |

No other files change. `ingress-websecure.yaml`, `kustomization.yaml`, and `deployment.yaml` remain untouched because the secret name is unchanged.

---

## Existing utilities reused

- `just validate` — offline kustomize build check (justfile:104)
- `just argocd-refresh` / `argocd-sync` / `argocd-status` / `argocd-diff` (justfile:178, 174, 170, 182)
- `just list-secrets` — sanity check that the secret is still included in the overlay (justfile:194)

No new scripts or recipes needed.

---

## Risks

1. **Downtime window is the critical variable.** Current cert expires 2026-05-11 — 21 days. Rotation must land before then. Surgical single-file change minimizes blast radius.
2. **Secret material in plain git.** Pre-existing pattern; rotation doesn't worsen it but doesn't fix it. Out-of-scope follow-up: SealedSecrets or External Secrets Operator.
3. **Wrong key/cert pairing** — mitigated by modulus-match check in Step 1.
4. **Missing intermediate in chain** — browsers/clients may reject leaf without `GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018`. We explicitly concatenate `CACert.crt` into the served chain.
5. **Passphrase exposure** — handled by `openssl rsa` interactive prompt; no chat transport of the secret.
6. **ArgoCD auto-sync behavior** — if auto-sync with `selfHeal` is on, step 5's manual sync is cosmetic. If prune/sync waves are configured, confirm the Secret is in the expected wave.

---

## Verification (end-to-end success criteria)

- `openssl x509 … notAfter` = `Nov 1 20:46:17 2026 GMT` on the decoded secret and on the live endpoint.
- `openssl verify` succeeds with `root.cer` trust anchor and `CACert.crt` intermediate.
- ArgoCD shows `Synced` and `Healthy` for the `charlie-server` application.
- `https://automacao.hypera.com.br` handshake succeeds with the new cert's dates.
- No churn in any file other than `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`.

---

## Where I stop (per timing decision)

I will execute **Steps 1 → 4 only** (stage artifacts → regenerate secret → local validate → commit + push). Then I **stop** and hand over. You trigger or wait for ArgoCD reconciliation (Step 5) and run post-deploy verification (Step 6) inside your maintenance window. I will remind you to run `shred -u` / cleanup (Step 7) after you confirm the rotation landed.

input.new_string:
### Step 1 — Obtain & stage the unencrypted key + cert chain (HIGH-RISK STEP)

This is the **single most sensitive step** in the entire rotation. The decrypted private key protects every HTTPS connection to `*.hypera.com.br`. Every line below is a safety control — please do not skip or reorder.

#### 🔒 Passphrase Safety Protocol — non-negotiable

| Control | Why it matters |
|---|---|
| **I (Claude) will NEVER ask you to type the passphrase into chat.** | Anything pasted here is stored in my conversation context — indefinite exposure. |
| **You type the passphrase directly into `openssl`'s TTY prompt.** | The prompt reads straight from `/dev/tty`; it bypasses shell history, process args, and env vars. |
| **Commands use `<` redirection, never `-passin pass:…` or env vars.** | `-passin pass:…` exposes the passphrase in `ps`, shell history, and `~/.zsh_history`. Hard ban. |
| **Scratch dir lives OUTSIDE the repo and OUTSIDE iCloud/Dropbox/OneDrive.** | Prevents accidental git commit AND prevents cloud sync from exfiltrating the key. |
| **`umask 077` before creating the scratch dir.** | Key file is mode `0600` — unreadable by other local users. |
| **Disable history in the shell session used for extraction.** | `unset HISTFILE` so even the *commands* (not just args) don't linger. |
| **Verify key/cert modulus match BEFORE using the key anywhere.** | If they don't match, we had the wrong `.pem` / wrong passphrase / corrupted file. Fail loud, fail early. |
| **Shred the decrypted key the moment it's no longer needed (Step 7).** | `rm` on APFS is a tombstone; `rm -P` overwrites. Don't rely on trim/TRIM for secrets. |
| **If the passphrase is wrong, STOP. Do not retry-loop.** | Brute-force retries suggest tooling is logging something. Pause, verify with cert issuer, resume. |

#### Exact commands (copy-paste into a fresh Terminal window)

```bash
# --- ONE-TIME SESSION HARDENING (this shell only) ---
unset HISTFILE              # kill history for this session
umask 077                   # new files get mode 0600

# --- SCRATCH DIR outside repo and outside any cloud folder ---
WORK=$(mktemp -d /tmp/hypera-cert-2026.XXXXXX)
cd "$WORK"
chmod 700 "$WORK"

# --- STAGE INPUTS (copies, so we don't mutate the delivered artifacts) ---
SRC="/absolute/path/to/certs/current"   # edit to the real absolute path
cp "$SRC/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem" src.pem
cp "$SRC/CACert.crt"                 intermediate.crt
cp "$SRC/root.cer"                   root.crt

# --- EXTRACT LEAF CERT (no passphrase needed; cert blocks aren't encrypted) ---
openssl x509 -in src.pem -out leaf.crt

# --- DECRYPT PRIVATE KEY ---
# openssl will print "Enter pass phrase for src.pem:" and read from your TTY.
# Type the passphrase by hand. It will NOT appear on screen.
# If you mistype: Ctrl-C, do NOT re-run with the passphrase in history. Just re-run `openssl rsa ...` again.
openssl rsa -in src.pem -out leaf.key

# --- IMMEDIATE INTEGRITY CHECK (must pass before we touch the repo) ---
KEY_MOD=$(openssl rsa  -in leaf.key  -noout -modulus | openssl md5)
CRT_MOD=$(openssl x509 -in leaf.crt  -noout -modulus | openssl md5)
[ "$KEY_MOD" = "$CRT_MOD" ] && echo "✅ key/cert modulus match" || { echo "❌ MISMATCH — STOP"; exit 1; }

# --- DATE + CN SANITY ---
openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -subject -issuer -startdate -enddate
# expect: subject=*.hypera.com.br, notAfter=Nov  1 20:46:17 2026 GMT

# --- CHAIN VALIDATES AGAINST ROOT + INTERMEDIATE ---
openssl verify -CAfile root.crt -untrusted intermediate.crt leaf.crt
# expect: leaf.crt: OK

# --- BUILD SERVED CHAIN (leaf + intermediate; root is NOT served) ---
cat leaf.crt intermediate.crt > fullchain.crt
```

> **Alternative path (if you later decide to use the `.pfx`):** we'd swap `openssl rsa -in src.pem -out leaf.key` for `openssl pkcs12 -in src.pfx -nocerts -nodes -out leaf.key` (PFX password prompt via TTY, same safety rules apply). Per your decision we're **not** using this path.

#### Hard gate before Step 2

Do **not** proceed to Step 2 unless ALL of the following are true:
- `✅ key/cert modulus match` printed.
- `leaf.crt: OK` printed from `openssl verify`.
- `notAfter=Nov  1 … 2026 GMT` confirmed.
- `ls -la leaf.key` shows `-rw-------` (mode 600).

If any check fails, **stop**, tell me what failed, and we diagnose before touching the repo.

# Plan — Rotate `hypera-ca-secret` to new 2026 wildcard certificate

## Context

The current TLS secret `hypera-ca-secret` terminates HTTPS for `automacao.hypera.com.br` via Traefik IngressRoute `charlie-server-websecure` (see `ingress-websecure.yaml:10`). The embedded certificate expires **2026-05-11** (≈21 days from today). A new wildcard cert `*.hypera.com.br` valid **2026-04-16 → 2026-11-01** has been delivered to `certs/current/` and must replace the in-repo secret so ArgoCD propagates it to the `charlie-prd` namespace.

**Why now:** the current cert expires in 3 weeks. We rotate in git so ArgoCD syncs the new secret to the cluster; Traefik picks up the updated secret automatically.

**Intended outcome:** `hypera-ca-secret.yaml` contains the new cert (chain) + matching unencrypted key as base64, commits cleanly to main, ArgoCD syncs, and `automacao.hypera.com.br` serves the new cert with `notAfter=Nov 1 2026`.

---

## Pre-flight facts verified

| Item | Current (decoded from `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`) | New (in `certs/current/`) |
|---|---|---|
| Subject CN | `*.hypera.com.br` | `*.hypera.com.br` ✅ same |
| Issuer | GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 | same ✅ |
| notBefore | 2025-10-30 | 2026-04-16 |
| notAfter | **2026-05-11** ⚠️ | 2026-11-01 |
| Key | Unencrypted RSA (PKCS#1) | **Encrypted** DES-EDE3-CBC (passphrase required) |

- Secret consumer: `ingress-websecure.yaml:10` only.
- Namespace (justfile default): `charlie-prd`.
- `*.pem` is in global `.gitignore` — cert source files won't be committed.
- `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem` contains: 1 encrypted RSA key + 3 certs (leaf, GlobalSign root, GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 intermediate).
- `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx` is PKCS#12, also password-protected.

---

## Confirmed decisions (from pre-plan Q&A)

1. **Key source:** decrypt `certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem` via `openssl rsa` — you have the passphrase and enter it interactively (never via chat).
2. **Secret type:** switch from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` (idiomatic K8s TLS type).
3. **ArgoCD auto-sync:** enabled for `charlie-server` — commit + push is sufficient; manual sync is cosmetic.
4. **Timing:** prepare the repo change today; trigger ArgoCD action during a planned maintenance window (I'll stop after push; you control the sync moment).

---

## Execution plan (step-by-step, you drive, I assist)

### Step 1 — Obtain & stage the unencrypted key + cert chain (LOCAL ONLY, secrets stay out of repo)

Work in a scratch dir outside the repo (e.g. `~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/`) to avoid any risk of committing secrets.

```bash
mkdir -p ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026
cd ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026

# Extract leaf certificate (first block in the .pem)
openssl x509 -in /absolute/path/to/certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem \
  -out leaf.crt

# Extract + decrypt private key (will prompt for passphrase)
openssl rsa \
  -in /absolute/path/to/certs/current/wildcard_hypera_com_br.pem \
  -out leaf.key
# ^ prompts: "Enter pass phrase for …:"

# OR, equivalent extraction from the PFX:
# openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -nocerts -nodes -out leaf.key
# openssl pkcs12 -in wildcard_hypera_com_br.pfx -clcerts -nokeys -out leaf.crt

# Build chain = leaf + intermediate (GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 = CACert.crt)
cat leaf.crt /absolute/path/to/certs/current/CACert.crt > fullchain.crt
```

**Verification (MUST pass before step 2):**
```bash
# Modulus match — key ↔ cert are a pair
diff <(openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -modulus | openssl md5) \
     <(openssl rsa  -in leaf.key  -noout -modulus | openssl md5)
# (no output = match)

# Dates and CN sanity
openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates
# expect: subject=*.hypera.com.br, notAfter=Nov 1 2026

# Chain validates
openssl verify -CAfile /absolute/path/to/certs/current/root.cer \
  -untrusted /absolute/path/to/certs/current/CACert.crt leaf.crt
# expect: leaf.crt: OK
```

### Step 2 — Generate the new `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`

From the repo worktree root. This overwrites the existing file with a dry-run–rendered Secret using the new artifacts.

```bash
kubectl create secret tls hypera-ca-secret \
  --cert=$HOME/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/fullchain.crt \
  --key=$HOME/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/leaf.key \
  --dry-run=client \
  -o yaml > hypera-ca-secret.yaml
```

Resulting file structure (per decision #2):
- `apiVersion: v1`, `kind: Secret`, **`type: kubernetes.io/tls`**
- `metadata.name: hypera-ca-secret` (no namespace — applied via Kustomize/ArgoCD)
- `data.tls.crt`: base64(leaf + `GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018` intermediate)
- `data.tls.key`: base64(unencrypted RSA key)

### Step 3 — Local validation (no cluster impact)

```bash
just validate                # kustomize build . OK
just build | yq 'select(.kind=="Secret" and .metadata.name=="hypera-ca-secret") | .data."tls.crt"' \
  | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026
```

### Step 4 — Commit & push (secrets committed to git ARE expected here; this is the existing pattern)

```bash
git status          # only hypera-ca-secret.yaml should appear
git add hypera-ca-secret.yaml
git commit -m "chore(tls): rotate hypera-ca-secret to 2026-11-01 wildcard cert"
git push
```

This repo already stores the TLS secret in plaintext git — we're matching existing practice. **Separately, we should open a follow-up to adopt SealedSecrets / SOPS / External Secrets** (noted in Risks, not part of this rotation).

### Step 5 — ArgoCD reconciliation (during your maintenance window)

Auto-sync is enabled — **no action needed from me after push**. During your maintenance window you can:

```bash
just argocd-status       # check it reconciled; expect Synced / Healthy
# Optional nudge if you don't want to wait for the next reconcile tick:
just argocd-refresh
just argocd-diff         # expect only hypera-ca-secret data changed
```

### Step 6 — Post-deploy verification (during your maintenance window)

```bash
# From inside cluster or wherever you have access
kubectl -n charlie-prd get secret hypera-ca-secret \
  -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.crt}' | base64 -d | \
  openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026

# From outside — real TLS handshake
echo | openssl s_client -servername automacao.hypera.com.br \
  -connect automacao.hypera.com.br:443 2>/dev/null | \
  openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates
# expect notAfter=Nov 1 2026
```

Traefik auto-reloads cert material on Secret changes; no pod restart required. If the served cert doesn't flip within ~60s, check the Traefik pod logs for reload events.

### Step 7 — Cleanup

```bash
shred -u ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026/leaf.key  # or `rm -P` on macOS
rm -rf ~/tmp/hypera-cert-2026
```

---

## Files modified

| Path | Change |
|---|---|
| `hypera-ca-secret.yaml` | Overwrite `data.tls.crt` (full chain) and `data.tls.key`; optionally flip `type` from `Opaque` → `kubernetes.io/tls` |

No other files change. `ingress-websecure.yaml`, `kustomization.yaml`, and `deployment.yaml` remain untouched because the secret name is unchanged.

---

## Existing utilities reused

- `just validate` — offline kustomize build check (justfile:104)
- `just argocd-refresh` / `argocd-sync` / `argocd-status` / `argocd-diff` (justfile:178, 174, 170, 182)
- `just list-secrets` — sanity check that the secret is still included in the overlay (justfile:194)

No new scripts or recipes needed.

---

## Risks

1. **Downtime window is the critical variable.** Current cert expires 2026-05-11 — 21 days. Rotation must land before then. Surgical single-file change minimizes blast radius.
2. **Secret material in plain git.** Pre-existing pattern; rotation doesn't worsen it but doesn't fix it. Out-of-scope follow-up: SealedSecrets or External Secrets Operator.
3. **Wrong key/cert pairing** — mitigated by modulus-match check in Step 1.
4. **Missing intermediate in chain** — browsers/clients may reject leaf without `GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018`. We explicitly concatenate `CACert.crt` into the served chain.
5. **Passphrase exposure** — handled by `openssl rsa` interactive prompt; no chat transport of the secret.
6. **ArgoCD auto-sync behavior** — if auto-sync with `selfHeal` is on, step 5's manual sync is cosmetic. If prune/sync waves are configured, confirm the Secret is in the expected wave.

---

## Verification (end-to-end success criteria)

- `openssl x509 … notAfter` = `Nov 1 20:46:17 2026 GMT` on the decoded secret and on the live endpoint.
- `openssl verify` succeeds with `root.cer` trust anchor and `CACert.crt` intermediate.
- ArgoCD shows `Synced` and `Healthy` for the `charlie-server` application.
- `https://automacao.hypera.com.br` handshake succeeds with the new cert's dates.
- No churn in any file other than `hypera-ca-secret.yaml`.

---

## Where I stop (per timing decision)

I will execute **Steps 1 → 4 only** (stage artifacts → regenerate secret → local validate → commit + push). Then I **stop** and hand over. You trigger or wait for ArgoCD reconciliation (Step 5) and run post-deploy verification (Step 6) inside your maintenance window. I will remind you to run `shred -u` / cleanup (Step 7) after you confirm the rotation landed.

+| **I (Claude) will NEVER ask you to type the passphrase into chat.** | Anything pasted here is stored in my conversation context — indefinite exposure. |
+| **You type the passphrase directly into `openssl`'s TTY prompt.** | The prompt reads straight from `/dev/tty`; it bypasses shell history, process args, and env vars. |
+| **Commands use `<` redirection, never `-passin pass:…` or env vars.** | `-passin pass:…` exposes the passphrase in `ps`, shell history, and `~/.zsh_history`. Hard ban. |

+| **Disable history in the shell session used for extraction.** | `unset HISTFILE` so even the *commands* (not just args) don't linger. |
+| **Verify key/cert modulus match BEFORE using the key anywhere.** | If they don't match, we had the wrong `.pem` / wrong passphrase / corrupted file. Fail loud, fail early. |
+| **Shred the decrypted key the moment it's no longer needed (Step 7).** | `rm` on APFS is a tombstone; `rm -P` overwrites. Don't rely on trim/TRIM for secrets. |
+| **If the passphrase is wrong, STOP. Do not retry-loop.** | Brute-force retries suggest tooling is logging something. Pause, verify with cert issuer, resume. |

input.new_string:
### Step 7 — Cleanup (run once post-deploy verification passes)

macOS uses APFS — `rm` doesn't actually overwrite. Use `rm -P` (BSD overwrite) on the key specifically, then nuke the dir.

```bash
# In the same scratch shell
rm -P "$WORK/leaf.key" "$WORK/src.pem"     # overwrite 3 passes before unlink
rm -rf "$WORK"
unset WORK KEY_MOD CRT_MOD                 # clear env vars
# Close this terminal window entirely — ensures no decrypted material lingers in scrollback.
```

Do NOT `shred` on macOS (it doesn't ship with GNU shred; `rm -P` is the BSD equivalent). On Linux, `shred -u` is fine.
